entry.title: New SAP Business Technology Platform (BTP) Offering for Educational Institutions
entry.content:
SAP BTP Build Sandbox for academia will be made available beginning of the school year 2024 (September 2024) by University Competence Center (UCC) Magdeburg globally

- This offer is only available for SAP University Alliances (UA) full members (Check out the details about the UA memberships in Latin America & Caribbean, Spain and Portugal) 

- Designed for using in the classroom teaching as well for try-out and research purposes with no time constraints

- This no/low-cost pilot offering provides academic users with flexibility in using default/starter content, build up on it or develop and upload their own content and share it with a wider community.

- SAP BTP Build Sandbox comprises a shared* subaccount of BTP Build with activated Build Process Automation and/or Build Apps services

- Currently deployed in the SAP Build Sandbox starter content includes teaching materials on SAP Build Process Automation (process robotization) and SAP Build Apps (LCNC).

*Exclusive subaccounts will be offered as a standard TLEC (Teaching and Learning Environment Contractor) offering with respective UCC fee of 600 Euros for a six-month access (for a semester). 

In case of questions/ comments send us an email via sapnextgen@sap.com or give us your comment here below! Happy usage! 

SAP University Alliances

entry.title: Devtoberfest 2024 Contest Official Rules
entry.content:
OFFICIAL RULES

NO PURCHASE REQUIRED TO ENTER OR TO WIN.
VALID ONLY IN ARGENTINA, AUSTRALIA, AUSTRIA, BELGIUM, CANADA (EXCLUDING QUEBEC), CHILE, CHINA, COLOMBIA, CZECH REPUBLIC, DENMARK, FINLAND, FRANCE, GERMANY, HONG KONG, HUNGARY, INDIA, INDONESIA, ISRAEL, JAPAN, MALAYSIA, MEXICO, NETHERLANDS, NEW ZEALAND, NORWAY, PERU, PHILIPPINES, POLAND, PORTUGAL, SINGAPORE, SOUTH AFRICA, SOUTH KOREA, SAUDI ARABIA, SPAIN, SWEDEN, SWITZERLAND, TAIWAN, THAILAND, TURKEY, UNITED ARAB EMIRATES, UNITED KINGDOM, THE UNITED STATES, AND VENEZUELA.
18 YEARS OF AGE OR OLDER AT TIME OF ENTRY. VOID WHERE PROHIBITED.

  1.  Eligibility: The Devtoberfest Game contest is open only to individuals who are legal residents of one of the eligible countries identified above, and who are at least 18 years of age at the time of their participation.

    - Employees, officers and representatives of SAP, and related companies, as well as their employees and immediate family (spouse, parents, siblings, and children) and household members of each such employee are eligible to participate but are not eligible to receive prizes other than nominal, non-cash prizes.

    - Employees of any SAP customers currently engaged in an active competitive bid cycle with SAP are not eligible to receive any prize in any tiers that exceeds $100, as determined by SAP.

    - Employees of the United States government or any foreign government, state, or territory, or any government affiliated entity, may participate, but are not eligible for prizes or gifts. This Devtoberfest Game contest is subject to all federal, state and local laws and regulations.

  2. Sponsor: SAP America, Inc., 3999 West Chester Pike, Newtown Square, PA 19073

  3. Agreement to Official Rules:  By joining the Devtoberfest Group and participating in the Devtoberfest Game contest, each entrant fully and unconditionally agrees to and accepts these Official Rules and the decisions of Sponsor in all matters relating to this Devtoberfest Game contest, which are final and binding. Entrants have the responsibility to review and understand their employer’s policies, laws, rules and/or regulations, tax implications, and any other limitations regarding eligibility to participate in promotions and/or receive prizes in connection therewith.  Furthermore, if you enter without obtaining the appropriate approvals, or if an individual is participating in violation of any such policies, Sponsor may, in its sole discretion, disqualify you and/or your entry, and forfeit the prize, if applicable.

  4. Timing: The Devtoberfest Game contest begins at 12:01:00 a.m. Pacific Time ("PT") September 23, 2024 and ends at 11:59:59 p.m. PT October 25, 2024 (the "Promotion Period").

  5. Complete Tutorials and Attend Devtoberfest Events to Earn Points. Eligible participants (as defined in Section 1, above) who connect to http://developers.sap.com and complete the designated individual tutorials and/or the “SAP Devtoberfest Mission”, which is a collection of tutorials specifically assembled for bonus points,  will be given badges with associated point values.  Participants can also attend special events over the 4 week Promotion Period that have been published on the Devtoberfest Group as being eligible to earn points during the Promotion Period.  Participation in SAP developer activities may be subject to additional terms.  Badges awarded with points will be published against the participant SAP Community ID and that will be used to verify their level of points achieved.  We will also have a “for entertainment purposes only” gameboard where participants can log in and see their progress up each level of a visual gameboard.  Point/Prize levels are: Level 1 requires at least 3000 points.  Level 2 requires at least 14000 points.  Level 3 requires at least 22000 points. Level 4 requires at least 30000 points.

    Sponsor is not responsible for typographical or system errors and failures or faulty transmissions, or for any problems or technical malfunction of servers or providers, computer equipment, or for any activity or information not registered or registered inaccurately  on account of technical problems or traffic congestion on the Internet, or at a website, or any combination thereof.

    Sponsor in its sole discretion may disqualify any entrant from participating in the Devtoberfest Game contest, refuse to award prize(s), and require the return of any prize(s), if entrant engages in any conduct Sponsor deems to be improper, unfair or otherwise adverse to the operation of the Devtoberfest Game contest or detrimental to other entrants. Such improper conduct includes, but is not limited to, falsifying personal information required during Devtoberfest Game contest registration or prize claim, violating any Official Rules stated herein, winning a prize(s) through methods such as automated computer scripts or other programming techniques, allowing others to use the entrant's personal information for the purpose of winning a Prize, or tampering with Sponsor's administration of this Devtoberfest Game contest.

  6. Prizes.  There can be an unlimited number of qualifiers across all four prize levels (described in Section 5, above). Participants that achieve these prize levels during the Promotion Period will have a chance to win prizes.  The prizes will be allocated by random drawing amongst the qualifiers at that prize level if the number of qualifiers exceeds the pool of prizes (described below) at any given prize level. Subject to the number of prizes available, as described in this section and below,  as you qualify for each level you win those prizes (or become eligible for the prize drawing at that level) in addition to the previous level.  For example, a level 3 winner also gets prizes in levels 1 and 2.
    Prize level 1 will be a digital Devtoberfest image valued at $0 USD once a point threshold of at least 3000 points is achieved. Prize level 2 is an electronic selfie with the developer advocates valued up to $0 USD once a point threshold of at least 14000 points achieved. Prize level 3 is an online Q&A session with an SAP leader valued at $0 USD once a point threshold of at least 22000 points achieved. Prize level 4 will be a chance to be one of 10 winners of an all-expense paid trip (see details below) to meet with SAP leaders, product experts and VIP experience once a point threshold of at least 30000 points achieved.

- Prize level 1 will have unlimited winners with approximate Retail Value of each prize $0 (zero) USD

- Prize level 2 will have unlimited winners with approximate Retail Value of each prize $0 (zero) USD

- Prize level 3 will have unlimited winners with approximate Retail Value of each prize $0 (zero) USD

- Prize level 4 will have up to 10 (ten) winners with approximate Retail Value of up to $5,000 USD per winner to include:
   * Economy round-trip airfare during event specific dates (the amount allocated for airfare will not exceed $5,000.)
   * Single Occupancy Hotel Room during event specific dates
   * Airport transfer fees to and from major gateway airports during event specific dates
   *  Meals during event specific dates
   *  Theme park ticket, meals, and transportation on specific date

If more entrants qualify in any given prize level in accordance with these Official Rules on or about October 31st, 2024, SAP will conduct a random drawing from all of the eligible participants at that prize level. The drawing will be conducted by Sponsor in accordance with these Official Rules, and the selection and determination of a winner(s) is final.  The entrant(s) selected as the winner(s) must comply with all terms and conditions of these Official Rules and winning is contingent upon fulfilling all requirements. Each winner will be notified using the contact information submitted when joining the Devtoberfest Group, and you consent to being contacted by Sponsor via any means available using this contact information for purposes of communicating with you about the Devtoberfest Game contest. If a potential winner cannot be contacted within three (3) days after the first attempt to contact him/her, Sponsor may at its sole and absolute discretion select an alternate entrant from among all eligible entries received for that prize category.

No cash or other substitution, assignment or transfer of prizes permitted, except by Sponsor, who reserves the right to substitute a prize or prize component with another of comparable or greater value. Winner is responsible for all taxes surcharges and fees associated with prize receipt and/or use.

IMPORTANT NOTICE FOR PRIZE LEVEL 4 (The Trip): All transportation, lodging, meals, gratuities, and all costs and expenses relating to or arising directly or indirectly out of the trip to be paid by SAP, will be determined by SAP. All costs not covered by SAP are the sole cost and responsibility of the winner.  The trip must be taken on dates and times specified by SAP, which dates and times are subject to change. SAP is not responsible if a selected winner is unable to attend the trip for any reason, and no additional or alternative or substitute prize or consideration will be given.  All travel arrangements must be made through a travel agent or entity selected by SAP. Winners must have or acquire a valid passport and/or visa for travel location of their own expense. Specific air carrier(s), lodging providers and other trip specifics are at the sole discretion of SAP. Travel must originate and end at major gateway airports specified by SAP. The trip winners will be required to provide valid travel documents and passports, valid photo IDs, and inoculations as required by travel authorities prior to and at time of travel; and to obtain and maintain all insurance (including health, liability and any other insurance). Winners acknowledge and agree that SAP is not a provider/carrier of transportation, or insurance provider. Winners may not make changes to travel details once any element(s) of the travel arrangements have been booked. SAP is not responsible for any changes of any element of the trip, including but not limited to any expenses incurred as a consequence of missed flights, flight charges, cancellation, or delays. SAP will not replace any lost or stolen tickets, travel vouchers, or certificates. Any unclaimed and/or unused element of the trip will be forfeited and remain the property of SAP.  In the event any trip winner engages in behavior that, as determined by SAP (or its designee) in their absolute discretion, is obnoxious or threatening, dangerous, illegal, disorderly, or that is intended to annoy, abuse, threaten or harass any other person, SAP reserves the right to terminate the trip early for that winner, in whole or in part, and to send that participant home with no further compensation. In the event that, for reasons beyond their control and not related to the Entrants, SAP is unable to award the trip as described in these Official Rules, SAP will substitute a prize of a similar nature and equal or greater value at their sole discretion.  The trip is non-assignable and non-transferable.  The trip is not redeemable for cash.  A winner is not a recipient of the trip prize until the winner has been  notified, responds, and compliance with the Rules and as a winner of the prize has been verified  by SAP.  If at any time a winner is unable to accept the prize in its entirety, the selected winner will forfeit the trip without any additional compensation or consideration, and the trip may be, at SAP’s discretion, awarded to an alternate winner.  All prize details are at SAP’s sole discretion.

Any prize awarded to you is awarded under the condition that your acceptance does not violate any applicable laws or policies within your organization. Public sector employees and/or government officials and SAP customers currently in an active competitive bid cycle with SAP are not eligible to receive prizes. Acceptance of this prize indicates that the recipient is neither public sector/government official or a customer in an active competitive bid with SAP.  If you are unsure whether your acceptance may violate any such laws or policies, we strongly encourage you to seek advice from your ethics or compliance official. If an individual or an organization is unable to accept all or a portion of a prize that is awarded, Sponsor may elect to award some portion of the prize to the recipient in question, or may elect to withdraw the prize and award it to another participant.

  1. Devtoberfest Game contest Administration/Publicity: Except where prohibited, participation in the Devtoberfest Game contest constitutes winner's consent to Sponsor’s use of winner's name, likeness, and biographical information for purposes of administering the Devtoberfest Game contest, including publication of information about winning entrants in accordance with applicable laws and regulations.  Use of personal information for advertising and promotional purposes will be subject to the consent of the participant.
  2. General Conditions: Sponsor reserves the right to cancel or modify the Devtoberfest Game contest if fraud, technical failures or any other factor beyond Sponsor's reasonable control impairs the integrity of the Devtoberfest Game contest, as determined by Sponsor in its sole discretion. In such event, Sponsor reserves the right to award the prizes at random from among the eligible entries received up to the time of the impairment. Sponsor reserves the right in its sole discretion to disqualify any individual it finds to be tampering with the entry process or the operation of the Devtoberfest Game contest or to be acting in violation of these Official Rules, or in an unsportsmanlike or disruptive manner. Sponsor's failure to enforce any term of these Official Rules shall not constitute a waiver of that provision.
  3. Release: By entering this Devtoberfest Game contest, entrant agrees to release and hold harmless, Sponsor, its related companies, its parent and affiliates and each of their respective officers, directors, employees, shareholders, and agents from and against any claim or cause of action arising out of participation in the Devtoberfest Game contest or receipt or use of any prize.
  4. Disputes/Limitation of Liability: Entrant agrees that: (i) any and all disputes, claims and causes of action arising out of or connected with this Devtoberfest Game contest, or any prizes awarded, other than the administration of the Devtoberfest Game contest or the determination of winners shall be resolved individually, without resort to any form of class action; (ii) any and all claims, judgments and awards shall be limited to actual out-of-pocket costs incurred, including costs associated with entering this Devtoberfest Game contest but in no event attorneys' fees; and (iii) under no circumstances will entrant be permitted to obtain awards for and entrant hereby waives all rights to claim punitive, incidental and consequential damages and any other damages, other than for actual out-of-pocket expenses, and any and all rights to have damages multiplied or otherwise increased. SOME JURISDICTIONS DO NOT ALLOW THE LIMITATIONS OR EXCLUSION OF LIABILITY FOR INCIDENTAL OR CONSEQUENTIAL DAMAGES, SO THE ABOVE MAY NOT APPLY TO YOU.
  5. Governing Law: All issues and questions concerning the construction, validity, interpretation and enforceability of these Official Rules, or the rights and obligations of the entrant and Sponsor in connection with the Devtoberfest Game contest, shall be governed by, and construed in accordance with, the laws of the State of New York, without giving effect to any choice of law or conflict of law rules (whether of the State of New York, or any other jurisdiction), which would cause the application of the laws of any jurisdiction other than the State of New York. In the event that any legal or regulatory authority challenges the Devtoberfest Game contest, Sponsor reserves the right to discontinue or modify the Devtoberfest Game contest, or to disqualify entrants residing in the affected geographic areas. In such event, Sponsor shall have no liability to entrants who are disqualified due to such an action.
  6. Use of Information: Sponsor will be collecting personal data about entrants online, in accordance with its privacy policy. Please review the Sponsor’s privacy policy at the link below. By participating in the Devtoberfest Game contest, entrants hereby agree to Sponsor’s collection and usage of their personal information and acknowledge that they have read and accepted Sponsor’s privacy policy at click here.
  7. Winners List/Official Rules: Devtoberfest Game contest results will be posted at http://community.sap.com/.

Grand Prize FAQ

entry.title: Devtoberfest 2024 Grand Prize Trip FAQ
entry.content:
How do I qualify for the Devtoberfest Grand Prize trip?
To learn how to qualify and earn points, please visit the official rules and regulations page of this site. For a complete listing of the contest activities as they are released each week of the event please see here: Coming Soon.

When does the contest begin and end?
The Grand Prize qualifications run from 12:01:00 a.m. Pacific Time ("PT") September 23, 2024 and ends at 11:59:59 p.m. PT October 25, 2024

Where will the 2024 Grand Prize be held?
The Grand Prize 2024 will be held at an SAP TechEd Tour Stop (to be determined) in the second half of 2025.

When is the Grand Prize trip?
The Grand Prize trip will take place in the second half of 2025 depending upon winner and SAP support staff availability and the timing of the SAP TechEd On Tour Stops. We will update this page when the official dates have been released.

If I qualify and want my family to come too, can I pay their way on the trip?
Yes, purchase options are available for additional guests, information will be available as the trip nears.

Will the SAP Executive Team be on the trip?
Yes! You will have the opportunity to spend time with SAP executives, product leaders, and innovators during the day, various meal functions, and other activities.

How frequently will the rank status be updated online?

Rankings/Standings are continuously updated, but some activities do have a few days lag before they show in the gameboard.  This gameboard is offered for entertainment purposes only. The actual points calculation for Devtoberfest levels and contest prizes will be done separately and could vary from the points displayed in this gameboard.  To check your latest ranking, add your SAP Community Profile ID to the end of the following URL: https://devrel-tools-prod-scn-badges-srv.cfapps.eu10.hana.ondemand.com/devtoberfestContest/scnId.Her....

Do I need a passport?
Each person attending will require a passport, valid for six months beyond the date of return and depending upon country of origin a valid visa to enter the United States.  Winners are responsible for acquiring their own passport and visa. 

Can I participate if I am based in another country?
Yes, this Grand Prize trip is open to all countries listed in the Official Rules. Final travel plans will be determinate upon having a valid passport and entry visas into the U.S.

When will the winners be notified?
Winners will be announced live at the SAP TechEd On Tour stop - ASUG Tech Connect 2024 the week of November 12th, 2024. Emails notifications will also be sent post the Event announcement.

How do I register for the Grand Prize trip?
Shortly after the winners are announced, you will receive an email notification with confirmation instructions. Please respond to the email in a very timely manner, you will be given a deadline. It’s important that you confirm your attendance on time.

Will I be able to receive a different award if I earn a spot on the Grand Prize Trip but am unable to attend?
No, the Grand Prize Trip cannot be redeemed for cash or points.

If I’m unable to attend and want to gift my Grand Prize trip to someone else, what should I do?
The Grand Prize Trip cannot be gifted to anyone else.

If I’m unable to attend but don’t want to gift my prize to someone else, whom should I inform?
If you can’t attend the Grand Prize trip, please respond to the confirmation notification email in a timely manner.

If I don’t qualify for the trip but still want to attend, can I pay my own way?
No, the Grand Prize trip is for qualified winners only.

entry.title: Devtoberfest 2024 Contest
entry.content:
By popular demand, we will once again have a contest aspect to Devtoberfest. In 2021 we introduced the idea of earning points from a variety of different activities. This expanded the contest to a much greater number of community members.  Therefore, this year’s Devtoberfest contest will again use the same basic structure.

 

 

It will be a points-based contest.  Points are awarded for things like attending one of the educational sessions, completing certain developer tutorials tied to the topic weeks, or other activities that contribute to the SAP Developer Community. Prizes are awarded based upon SAP Community badges which you earn and you must have a public Community profile to participate.
 
The listing of activities, points, and other contest details is Coming Soon.
 
But the fun doesn’t end there.  We brought back the animated Contest Gameboard to help you track your progress through the contest.  

For these services to work you will need to know your SAP Community ID. You will, of course, need to be signed into the Community. If so, you can click on the person silhouette in the upper right corner and choose My content. This will navigate you to your profile overview as well as where you can configure settings within the SAP Community.

The SAP Community ID is an integer that you can find at the end of URL of your public profile page.

 

Then just add this integer to the end of the following URL: https://devrel-tools-prod-scn-badges-srv.cfapps.eu10.hana.ondemand.com/devtoberfestContest/scnId.Her....
 
And if you are curious how we built the animated gameboard, we have also released the whole thing as a Sample Code repository here.
 

 

We also have digital prizes that you can win this year. But most exciting, we have a genuinely great grand prize again this year. It's an all-expense paid trip (see details below) to one of the SAP TechEd Tour Stops in 2025 to meet with SAP leaders, product experts and an VIP experience at one of area's major tourist destinations. This grand prize includes:
  • Economy round-trip airfare during event specific dates
  • Single Occupancy Hotel Room during event specific dates
  • Airport transfer fees to and from major gateway airports during event specific dates
  • Meals during event specific dates
  • Theme Park ticket, meals, and transportation on specific date
You will need to earn points to qualify for a prize tier and then to be as fair as possible we will perform a random drawing amongst everyone that qualified for that level.  And as always be sure to review the legal terms and conditions. We also have an FAQ About the Grand Prize Trip.
 
All the possible points for earning activities and the value of each activity are detailed here (Coming Soon).
 
Complete tutorials, attend sessions, and solve code challenges you might be sporting a nice pair of mouse ears on your VIP prize trip!

entry.title: SAP Integration Suite Cloud Integration Utilities - Upload Value Mapping Entries based on JSON/XML
entry.content:
SAP Integration Suite Cloud Integration Utilities - Is a community package for Integration Suite provided by SAP consultants.

 

This package contains selected flows and scripts that can be leveraged in daily business operations.

In this blog post we focus on the flow: Upload Value Mapping Entries based on JSON or XML Input

Background:

  • Checking the Integration Content for Integration Suite in SAP Business Accelerator Hub there is also a Value Mapping API available with all the different facets of operation for creation, update, and deletion.
  • A major point - the creation of a new Value Mapping - always requires an upload of the content as Base64 Zip-File
  • Even for update or deletion mutiple apis calls are required
  • This leads to the question: Is there a more convenient way of usage?

Solution: 

The flow in the utilities package allows:

  • Upload of a new VM based on a JSON or XML payload
  • Update existing VM based on a JSON or XML payload (see insights)
  • Deletion and undeployment an existing VM based on a JSON or XML payload

Insights:

  • First time creation: the flow creates in memory a ZIP-File and does a converting to base64 string based on the incoming payload - (Hint: you can use the script also for your own purpose - adjust it :))
  • Flow control by HTTP-Method (Post, Put, Delete) or via action in payload: (create, update, delete)
  • Important:
    • During creation all combinations of Source Agency/Identifier and Target Agency/Identifier etc. will be created
    • During update of VM only available value sets of Source Agency/Identifier and Target Agency/Identifier will be updated with new combinations of source and target values, a manual creation for this additional Source Agency/Identifier and Target Agency/Identifier is required – all values can be uploaded via API

Payload:

 

{
    "name": "VMTEST",
    "packageid": "PackageID",
    "description": "Upload via API",
    "action": "create",
    "_comment": "action: create also as HTTP-POST-Method, update also as HTTP-PUT-Method, delete also as HTTP-DELETE-Method",
    "items": [
        {
            "sourceagency": "Source Agency",
            "sourceidentifier": "Source Identifier",
            "targetagency": "Target Agency",
            "targetidentifier": "Target Identifier",
            "items": [
                {
                    "source": "source value new 1",
                    "target": "target value new 2"
                },
                {
                    "source": "source value3",
                    "target": "target value4"
                }
            ]
        },
        {
            "sourceagency": "Source Agency1",
            "sourceidentifier": "Source Identifier1",
            "targetagency": "Target Agency1",
            "targetidentifier": "Target Identifier1",
            "items": [
                {
                    "source": "source value5",
                    "target": "target value6"
                },
                {
                    "source": "source value7",
                    "target": "target value8"
                }
            ]
        }
    ]
}

 

Results:

during creation:

  • VM created: "VMTEST" as Version "1.0.0"

  • according to payload, values created in VM

during update:

  • Version incremented: "1.0.0" to "1.0.1"

  • according to payload, 4 pairs added

entry.title: Recap Blog: SAP Stammtisch Plus in Hyderabad - August 3, 2024
entry.content:
The SAP Stammtisch Plus event held on August 3, 2024, in Hyderabad was a resounding success, bringing together a vibrant community of SAP enthusiasts and professionals. With 30 attendees, the event was a perfect blend of insightful sessions, networking opportunities, and engaging discussions. Here's a detailed recap of the key sessions and highlights from the event.

Event Overview

The SAP Stammtisch Plus in Hyderabad was designed to foster community engagement, share knowledge, and explore the latest trends and innovations in the SAP ecosystem. The positive feedback from attendees underscores the event's success and the enthusiasm for future gatherings in Hyderabad.

Welcome Speech by Vasavi

The day began with a warm and inspiring welcome speech by Vasavi, Senior Director and Head of SAP Hyderabad. Vasavi expressed her excitement at seeing such an enthusiastic turnout and emphasized the importance of community and collaboration in driving innovation and excellence. Her words set the stage for a day filled with learning and networking.

Key Sessions

  1. SAP Community and Its Roadmap - Ruthvik Chowdary & Poonam

Ruthvik Chowdary and Poonam kicked off the sessions with an enlightening discussion on the future direction of SAP community events and developments. They highlighted the importance of community engagement and shared the roadmap for upcoming SAP initiatives. Attendees appreciated the insights into how they can contribute to and benefit from the SAP community.

2. Advanced Returns Management - Priyanka Vanjarapu

Priyanka Vanjarapu's session on Advanced Returns Management was a deep dive into strategies for optimizing returns processes. She discussed various techniques to enhance efficiency and reduce costs, providing practical examples and case studies. The session was particularly beneficial for professionals looking to streamline their returns management processes.

3. Nuts & Bolts of AI - Dr. Mahesh Kumar CV

Dr. Mahesh Kumar CV captivated the audience with his session on the "Nuts & Bolts of AI." He spoke about AI use cases and business examples, demonstrating the transformative potential of AI in various industries. The session was both informative and inspiring, sparking numerous discussions among attendees about the future of AI in business.

4. SAP Territory and Quota Management - Geetha & Phani

Geetha & Phani's session on SAP Territory and Quota Management provided valuable insights into effective territory and quota management for improved sales performance. He shared best practices and tools that can help organizations optimize their sales strategies. Attendees found the session highly relevant and actionable.

5. SAP GenAI Use Cases in SAP SD - Aman Sharief

Aman Sharief concluded the event with a compelling presentation on SAP GenAI use cases in SAP SD. He showcased three sales Gen AI use cases and their architecture, offering a glimpse into how AI can revolutionize sales processes. The session was well-received, with attendees eager to explore these innovative solutions further.

Acknowledgements

The success of the SAP Stammtisch Plus event in Hyderabad would not have been possible without the meticulous planning and organization by the core team and volunteers. Their hard work and dedication ensured the seamless execution of the event, making it a memorable experience for all attendees.

Conclusion

The SAP Stammtisch Plus event in Hyderabad was a testament to the power of community and collaboration. The diverse range of sessions provided valuable insights and sparked meaningful conversations among attendees. The overwhelming positive feedback and enthusiasm for future events highlight the success of this gathering.

We look forward to hosting more such events in Hyderabad and continuing to build a strong and engaged SAP community. Thank you to all the speakers and attendees for making this event a memorable one!

Stay tuned for updates on upcoming SAP events and initiatives.

Thanks & Regards
Ruthvik Chowdary

entry.title: SAP Inside Track: Youngsters need challenges
entry.content:
Viktor Frankl, the renowned Austrian neurologist, psychiatrist, and Holocaust survivor, believed in the profound importance of finding meaning in life, a central theme in his influential work, “Man’s Search for Meaning.” He asserted that one of the key ways to foster a sense of purpose and fulfillment, especially among young people, is to provide them with challenges.

Frankl argued that facing and overcoming challenges is essential for personal growth and development. He believed that when young people encounter difficulties, they are given opportunities to discover their inner strengths, develop resilience, and cultivate a sense of responsibility and purpose. By navigating these challenges, they can build character and gain a deeper understanding of themselves and their potential.

Viktor Frankl’s philosophy on the necessity of challenges for personal growth can be aptly applied to the context of SAP Inside Track Events, particularly for new SAP consultants seeking a professional purpose. Just as Frankl emphasized the importance of challenges for discovering inner strengths and building resilience, participating, organizing, and volunteering at these events offers new consultants a meaningful way to share knowledge and contribute to the community that supports them in their daily work.

Viktor Frankl’s Perspective Applied to SAP Inside Track Events:

1. Facing Challenges Through Participation
Frankl believed that challenges are essential for personal development. For new SAP consultants, participating in SAP Inside Track Events provides an opportunity to face professional challenges head-on. By engaging with complex topics, presenting ideas, and sharing experiences, they can push their boundaries and expand their expertise.

2. Finding Purpose in Organizing
Organizing these events is a significant undertaking that requires dedication, planning, and teamwork. According to Frankl, finding purpose often involves contributing to something greater than oneself. By taking on the responsibility of organizing an SAP Inside Track Event, consultants can experience the fulfillment that comes from creating a platform for knowledge sharing and community building.

3. Volunteering as a Means of Giving Back
Volunteering at these events is a way for new consultants to give back to the community that supports them. Frankl emphasized that meaning in life is created through acts of service and contribution. By volunteering their time and skills, consultants can make a positive impact on their peers, fostering a sense of camaraderie and mutual support within the SAP ecosystem.

4. Building Resilience and Professional Growth
Engaging in these activities helps new consultants develop resilience and professional growth. The challenges they encounter will help them develop problem-solving skills, enhance their communication abilities, and build confidence in their professional capabilities. This aligns with Frankl’s belief that overcoming difficulties leads to a deeper understanding of one’s strengths and potential.

Conclusion
In the spirit of Viktor Frankl’s philosophy, new SAP consultants are encouraged to participate, organize, and volunteer at SAP Inside Track Events. These activities not only provide professional purpose and opportunities to share knowledge but also allow consultants to give back to the community that supports them. By embracing these challenges, they can grow personally and professionally, ultimately finding deeper meaning and fulfillment in their careers.

References: Youtube interview

entry.title: Announcing the Winner of "My Most Clever Response in a Job Interview” Contest
entry.content:
You're sitting in a job interview, nerves on edge, trying to impress the interviewer with your skills and expertise. Suddenly, a question catches you off guard, and you find yourself scrambling for the perfect response. We've all been there! We asked you to showcase your moments of brilliance and celebrate the power of a clever response – and would now like to thank everyone who entered the contest.

During the past month, we’ve been intrigued by your honesty and many tips – and now it’s time to announce our winner!!!

Congratulations to @KunalBansal 

We were all very impressed with your insights!!

Winning Entry: Be Authentic, Comfortable and Interview the Interviewer 🧑🏻‍💻

In my last interviews, which consisted of mainly three rounds - Technical, Managerial, and HR - I experienced a moment that truly showcased the power of a clever response.

After successfully demonstrating my technical competence in the 1st round, I moved on to the managerial interview with the practice lead.

The discussion was focused on my understanding of business processes, technical expertise, and experience with previous organizations to which I answered authentically and confidently with some cross-questions, establishing my credibility and expertise.

I never hesitated to use the whiteboard to explain my understanding of any topic. (off-course taking permission to use it and wiping board at the end)

As the 45 mins interview drew close, the practice lead asked the classic question: Do you have any questions for me? (This moment can significantly impact the interview outcome, and I seized the opportunity to make a lasting impression.)

I asked some smart questions about the overall practice strength, org-structure, project pipeline, team dynamics, and my potential role and responsibilities.

By visualising myself in the position already and expressing genuine curiosity, I showed my sincere interest in the organisation, intending to understand how the team is structured, the types of projects handled, and how well I would fit into the team.

I also asked if I needed to prepare for any specific skills before joining.

Lastly, I asked him about his overall professional journey, fostering a positive and engaging conversation and we closed the interview on a very positive note followed by an offer.

Bottom line: If we can genuinely interview the interviewer, it showcases intent, confidence, knowledge, and authenticity which leaves a strong impression and reinforces genuine interest in joining the team and org.

Feel free to add comments share your views or any suggestions.

Thank you.

By @KunalBansal  (SAP Champion and CX Architect)

 

Let me also highlight two other entries that received many kudos by community members.

2nd Place Entry: Turning Stress into Success!!

Few questions during the interviews looks so simply but it’s really difficult to answer sometimes. One of such question I faced during an interview is,

What is your greatest strength?

At my last job, we had a major project deadline approaching, and the team was feeling the pressure. Sensing the tension, I jokingly suggested we should start a 'Survivor: Office Edition' to see who could last the longest without coffee. Everyone laughed, and it broke the ice. It inspired me a bit and I organized a spontaneous 'innovation hour' where we brainstormed creative solutions. One idea was so good it saved us hours of work. So, my greatest strength I should say turning stress into success with a bit of humour and some unconventional thinking.

By @bhavinvyas 

3rd Place Entry: My Most Clever Response in a Job Interview

Many moons ago, one of my managers gave me this great piece of advice.  He told me at any job interview, when they ask you at the end of the interview, "do you have any final questions" you ask this:

Is there anything about me or my background that wouldn't make me a good fit for this job?

After asking that, I have always received a job offer except one time.  It's a great question, and I hope you use it successfully like I did.

By @TammyPowlas 

As the winner, @KunalBansal will receive an SAP merchandise voucher worth 40 euros!

A big shout out to all the great entries! All contest participants will be given the “Clever Interviewee” badge for your community profile in the coming days.
@KunalBansal @Hemalatha7 @Florian @Sarfaraz_Shaikh @adripb @swapson @sneha_nayak
 @bhavinvyas @TammyPowlas @NRamya @MichaelZalewski @Dharini_1912 

Congrats once again to @KunalBansal 

SAP Community Team

Keen to take part in the next contest? Stay tuned to our Coffee Corner!

entry.title: Sharing Power, Doubling Success - That's Co-Leadership @ SAP! 🚀 #5 Tandem
entry.content:
Discovering Success in Global Co-Leadership: Rachel and Iris’s Journey

Welcome to the latest edition of our enlightening blog series on Co-Leadership! Today, we are excited to introduce Rachel Winship and Iris Müller, our fifth Co-Leadership duo. With over a year of experience co-leading as Heads of Global People Compliance, Rachel and Iris have demonstrated the power and effectiveness of shared leadership.

Based in Bellevue, Washington, USA, and Walldorf, Germany, respectively, Rachel and Iris present a compelling example of a successful global partnership. Join us as we delve into their journey and discover the unique Co-Leadership lessons they have learned along the way.

 

Why Co-Leadership?

Rachel shared that she and Iris were offered the Co-Leadership model after participating in the interview process for their role. During their initial meeting to get to know each other, they instantly clicked and recognized how well their skills and backgrounds complemented each other. Rachel brings a legal background and experience in investigations, while Iris has extensive experience at SAP, having already served as a manager and worked closely with the Works Council. Additionally, Rachel's expertise in navigating the complexities of the U.S. market adds another dimension to their dynamic partnership. This blend of strengths made the Co-Leadership model an ideal choice for them.

 

How do you delegate work & responsibilities?

In our interview, Rachel described their leadership style as more of a horizontal structure. They have divided their team by region according to the organizational chart, allowing each of them to conduct weekly one-on-one meetings with employees in their respective regions. Additionally, they meet monthly with employees from other regions to ensure a cohesive team environment. As managers they keep each other updated on the well-being and progress of their team members. When necessary, they attend meetings together with an employee to provide unified support.

Communication is a key aspect of their partnership; they speak daily, sometimes only via Microsoft Teams. Their effective collaboration allows them to discuss matters on an ad hoc basis as needed. To stay organized, they share a OneNote where they can log in and access information as required. Often, they split meetings based on time zones to manage their global team efficiently. This approach has fostered a strong partnership and a well-coordinated leadership dynamic.

 

How did you navigate the process of determining and dividing your responsibilities as Co-Leaders?

Rachel told us that they reached a consensus almost immediately thanks to an atmosphere of open dialogue. She attributes this success to their open-mindedness, mutual respect for each other's skills and experiences, and their willingness to engage in open discussions.

 

The Impact of Co-Leadership

Rachel expressed her immense gratitude for the knowledge she has gained from Iris, especially in areas where she herself had less experience, such as team management and liaising with the Works Council.

“I think Co-leadership positively affects our team dynamics because between the two of us we have a vast amount of experience and a wide range of skills.” 

Rachel has observed that their Co-Leadership model greatly boosts their team dynamics. The combination of their extensive experience and diverse skill sets empowers them to effectively tackle a wide variety of challenges. Moreover, their geographically dispersed locations – Rachel is based on the west coast of the US while Iris is in Germany – enables them to efficiently cover multiple time zones.

She is confident that their team has a positive view of the Co-Leadership structure. The team observes them supporting and collaborating with one another to reach their team’s objectives, which fosters an environment of mutual respect and collective success.

 

How did you overcome challenges within your Co-Leadership?

Rachel shared with us that their experience in the Co-Leadership role has been largely smooth and that they hadn’t had many challenges during their joint leadership journey. One potential obstacle is the time difference between their locations. While this difference can be often advantageous, it occasionally makes scheduling overlapping hours for discussions a bit challenging.

“Flexibility has been key.” 

However, both Rachel and Iris tackle this issue by being flexible. Rachel said that she is willing to start her day very early if necessary, and Iris adjusts by scheduling conversations later in her day. This flexibility and adaptability have been essential to their success.

 

 

We greatly appreciate Rachel for sharing her valuable time and insights about her Co-Leadership experience together with Iris. 🌟

Our blog series delves into various Co-Leadership scenarios at SAP, highlighting the benefits for both the managers and the organization. Additionally, we seek to provide practical insights that might inspire other employees to consider embracing Co-Leadership.

Take a moment to explore our previous articles and gain valuable perspectives:

#1 What is Co-Leadership? Co-Leadership as a Pathway to New Work? SAP Community / LinkedIn

#2 Our first Co-Leadership Duo – Melanie & Mathias SAP Community / LinkedIn

#3 Pairing up for Success: Story of Rebekka and Simon SAP Community / LinkedIn

#4 Co-Leadership Spotlight: Unveiling the Leadership Journey of Elena and Jens SAP Community / LinkedIn

#5 The Leadership Path of Irene and Daniela SAP Community / LinkedIn

entry.title: Sharing Power, Doubling Success - That's Co-Leadership @ SAP! 🚀 #6 Tandem
entry.content:
Celebrating Four Years of Dynamic Co-Leadership: Meet Kirsten & Jörg

In an era where shared leadership is becoming a key catalyst for innovation and success, we are excited to introduce our sixth exceptional Co-Leadership team: Kirsten Mollet and Jörg Freiler. For nearly four years, this dynamic duo has been at the Head of Premium Engagements Switzerland, leading with synergy and shared vision. Operating from the charming city of Zürich, Switzerland, Kirsten and Jörg are fully committed to the roles they have shaped together. Their partnership stands as one of the longest-running Co-Leadership teams featured in our blog series to date.

 

Why Co-Leadership?

When we asked Kirsten why she chose the Co-Leadership model, her answer was rooted in a significant moment four years ago. "My former manager resigned from SAP, and I was asked if I would be willing to take over the team." At the time, she was deeply engaged with another customer, and the sudden leadership shift posed a unique set of challenges.

In order to manage the transition smoothly and ensure robust leadership for a growing team, they opted for the Co-Leadership model. Initially responsible for a team of 24, which has now expanded to 28, Kirsten realized that single-handedly balancing the demands would have been overwhelming.

When they chose this Co-Leadership model, a few strategic priorities guided their decision. Firstly, she wanted to stay closely to the job. Maintaining a hands-on approach was crucial for her to stay in touch with core activities and the evolving needs of the customers. Additionally, she emphasized the importance of continuing to support her sales colleagues.

Leading a team is an ongoing process of growth and development, and she recognized that she needed the capacity to do it effectively. Thus, the Co-Leadership model appeared to be the best solution. Focusing on strategic priorities was another pillar that underscored their choice. The model provided the bandwidth for both Kirsten and Jörg to concentrate on long-term goals without losing sight of their daily responsibilities.

Now, four years into this journey, Kirsten and Jörg's Co-Leadership model has proven to be an outstanding success. Their shared leadership was not merely a practical solution but a well-thought-out strategy - demonstrating the effectiveness of their collaborative approach.

 

How do you delegate work & responsibilities?

When we asked Kirsten about how she and Jörg handle the delegation and distribution of work within their Co-Leadership tandem, she began with a fundamental principle: "We have established a horizontal leadership structure." This approach fosters a culture of shared responsibility and mutual respect.

Does the distribution of tasks depend on time constraints? "Yes, it does," Kirsten confirmed, "but it's not just about managing time. It's also influenced by our relationships, background and knowledge." This nuanced approach allows them to leverage their unique strengths and areas of expertise, ensuring that tasks are assigned to those best equipped to handle them.

Both of them are engaged in all aspects of leadership, maintaining consistency and seamless team functioning. They have not divided themselves into professional and personal leadership roles. But one critical aspect of their leadership model is how they interact with their team members. Kirsten clarified that they have divided the team when it comes to 1:1 meetings to ensure that each team member receives focused attention and individualized support. However, for significant conversations, such as salary discussions, they always conduct these together. This dual approach combines the benefits of individualized attention with the strength of united decision-making for critical matters.

 

How did you navigate the process of determining and dividing your responsibilities as Co-Leaders?

When we asked Kirsten about the process of agreeing on Co-Leadership responsibilities and task division, she shared, "We have very different personalities and backgrounds. It took some months until we were fully aligned." These initial challenges stemmed from the diverse perspectives each brought to the table, making consensus a gradual achievement.

To overcome these differences, Kirsten emphasized the importance of communication. "Honest, open, and transparent communication was key," she explained. Through consistent and open discussions, they were able to gradually find common ground and streamline their collaborative efforts.

Today, their hard work has paid off. "Now, we absolutely enjoy working together," Kirsten said. "Every day, we benefit from our different skills and perspectives.” Their distinctive strengths complement each other, making their leadership style robust and dynamic.

Although the path to alignment wasn't straightforward, it was ultimately rewarding and transformative. Through open dialogue and a willingness to adapt, Kirsten and Jörg have established a harmonious Co-Leadership dynamic that capitalizes on their diverse skills and backgrounds.

 

The Impact of Co-Leadership

When asked to identify the positive changes resulting from their Co-Leadership role, Kirsten was quick to highlight the benefits. "We have enough time to work on different tasks," she noted. "Our varied backgrounds and leadership styles allow us to act whenever needed, ensuring we can tackle a wide range of challenges effectively."

Kirsten emphasized how Co-Leadership positively affects their team dynamics. "The team really appreciates having both of us as managers," she said. The diverse perspectives Kirsten and Jörg bring to the table contribute to a balanced and responsive leadership approach, which has resonated well with their team members. The positive benefits of Co-Leadership have been also clearly reflected in the #Unfiltered results of SAP’s annual employee survey.

 

How did you overcome challenges within your Co-Leadership?

When asked how they overcame challenges in their Co-Leadership role, Kirsten's answer was clear: "Open and honest communication - no hiding, no gossiping." This core principle has been the cornerstone of their leadership success, allowing them to navigate obstacles effectively and maintain a strong partnership.

In reflecting on their journey, Kirsten expressed heartfelt appreciation for Jörg. "Thank you, Jörg, for the previous 4 years!" she said warmly. "And here’s to at least 4 more years!" This gratitude underscores the mutual respect and trust that have developed over time, turning challenges into opportunities for growth.

 

We greatly appreciate Kirsten for sharing her valuable time and insights about her Co-Leadership experience together with Jörg. 🌟

 

Our blog series delves into various Co-Leadership scenarios at SAP, highlighting the benefits for both managers and the organization. Additionally, we seek to provide practical insights that might inspire other employees to consider embracing Co-Leadership.

Take a moment to explore our previous articles and gain valuable perspectives:

#1 What is Co-Leadership? Co-Leadership as a Pathway to New Work? SAP Community / LinkedIn

#2 Our first Co-Leadership Duo – Melanie & Mathias SAP Community / LinkedIn

#3 Pairing up for Success: Story of Rebekka and Simon SAP Community / LinkedIn

#4 Co-Leadership Spotlight: Unveiling the Leadership Journey of Elena and Jens SAP Community/ LinkedIn

#5 The Leadership Path of Irene and Daniela SAP Community / LinkedIn

#6 Discovering Success in Global Co-Leadership: Rachel and Iris’s Journey SAP Community / LinkedIn 

#7 Celebrating Four Years of Dynamic Co-Leadership: Meet Kirsten & Jörg

entry.title: Business AI Integration SAP Transaction Monitoring with Acceleration APIs My Experience as a Speaker
entry.content:
Abstract:

In the SAP Integration Suite, message monitoring reports are essential for tracking transactions. This blog provides practical insights for developers, emphasizing how Business Acceleration Hub APIs can enhance reporting efficiency based on real-world experience.

Introduction:

Many organizations depend on daily transaction reports, but issues such as report disappearance can be frustrating. The SAP Integration Suite’s message monitoring tool is invaluable for tracking failed transactions, but managing these reports can be challenging. With Artificial Intelligence (AI) and Integration Suite APIs, you can proactively detect issues and simplify operations.

Agenda:

Cost Background: Zero-cost options for email reports and AI suggestions.

Overview: The inefficiencies of manual report generation and its impact.

Technical Implementation: Step-by-step integration details for automation and AI.

Experience Highlight: My experience with SAP SIT-2024 as Speaker and practical insights.

Conclusion: Key takeaways and future directions.

Technical Implementation:

Setup: Obtain API keys, enable Integration Suite API, and gather mail server credentials.

Integration Steps:

Configure Parameters and Queries: Set up parameters and use Groovy scripts to filter statuses and retrieve messages.

Handle Responses: Use Iteration Splitter to process and query additional APIs.

Leverage AI: Utilize OpenAI models to provide error suggestions and integrate these with the report content.

Report Generation: Gather responses, map messages, and generate HTML reports. Dispatch these via email using the Integration Suite’s mail adapter.

Experience As a Speaker Highlight:

As a speaker at SAP SIT-2024 on July 6th, 2024, I shared practical insights on integrating Business Acceleration Hub APIs with SAP Integration Suite, demonstrating effective solutions for enhancing transaction monitoring and reporting efficiency.

Conclusion:

Incorporating AI and Business Acceleration Hub APIs into SAP Integration Suite reporting can significantly enhance efficiency. These technologies simplify error handling, improve report reliability, and expedite issue resolution. By embracing these advancements, organizations can optimize their reporting processes and focus more on strategic activities.

Additional Resources:

For more details, refer to the Integration Suite API documentation and the AI suggestion integration guide.

entry.title: SAP Social Sabbatical – Reflections & Gratitude
entry.content:
Just before SAP Sapphire in Orlando this year, I returned from my SAP Social Sabbatical program in Kenya. I am sharing with you my take on this incredible program because I realize that many are not familiar with it, yet the program presents an amazing opportunity for all SAP catalysts and executives to drive positive change - both within our communities and for our personal and professional growth.

SAP Social Sabbatical is an award-winning portfolio of global volunteering consultancy programs that empower SAP employees to share their knowledge, skills, and expertise with non-profits and social enterprises all around the world. In its purest form, the social enterprise is a real, income-generating business, but one that has a clear social mission. It is a pioneering and burgeoning sector, numbering in the millions worldwide.
SAP is not a social enterprise, but we are inspired by them and our Social Sabbatical program is an opportunity to engage with these inspiring ventures and their visionary founders. It is also an opportunity to experience SAP's purpose - to help the world run better and improve people’s lives - on a very tacit and personal level.

To date, the program has positively impacted 7M+ lives, helped create 460+ jobs, and supported 531 organizations worldwide through 1,601 SAP employees, of which 47% have been our women colleagues. Within it, there are various global, regional, and local programs tailored for SAP executives and the talent in the Catalyst Development Program, so everyone can find an opportunity to volunteer for something suited to their skillset

Personally, I had stepped away from my day-to-day responsibilities for two weeks to participate in the Kenya program, but I came back with years of energy and positivity to give back to our business, our team, SAP and the communities we serve. That amount of motivation, drive and purpose, is the tangible outcome of our Social Sabbatical program I would like all of you to experience.

I met very young people who already know their potential, and who are incredibly capable. They are executing very strategic world-class programs and leveraging technology and AI as much as some of the most capable in our teams in SAP today. But, while globalization and technology have made education more accessible to all over the last couple of decades, they couldn’t offer access to equal local and global opportunities at the same pace. SAP stepped up to help through the best we have to offer – access to markets, our technology and our incredible and skilled talent.

I supported a social enterprise called Taka Taka Ni Mali, founded by one of Kenya’s most successful women entrepreneurs who was named the Woman Entrepreneur of the Year in Kenya in 2018. The name of her enterprise in Swahili means ‘Waste is Wealth’ as the business focuses on managing and recycling waste, with a vision to turn Kenya into an environmentally sustainable nation. The business relies on donors to fund their enterprise, but Mary, the founder, is intent on making Taka Taka Ni Mali a financially independent enterprise with sustainable long-term success.

We were tasked with helping the business explore monetization opportunities to self-fund its social mission. Two weeks later, two of my SAP colleagues and I, representing three different SAP Board areas, redesigned Taka Taka Ni Mali’s entire business model, identifying five key monetization opportunities, and building the financial model and business plan for two of them. The plan was adopted, and the execution started while we were still in Nairobi!
These two weeks had a massive impact on my personal growth too.

Being taken out of my comfort zone - from the daily hum and drum of the business world - and being thrown into a problem that affected the lives of the people in front of me, brought a new level of clarity, efficiency and personal motivation to resolve that problem! The importance of finding a personal connection between the problem and my motivation to solve it, is one of the key lessons from this experience for me.

I was also successfully reminded about the power of conviction – seeing the determination and the absence of fear to go after one’s conviction even in such challenging environments, reignited my personal conviction in the journey we have charted for our Digital motion. I come back stoked, knowing that a few determined individuals can indeed move mountains. We, on the other hand, are a +1,000 men and woman-strong team in the Customer Success Digital motion – our goals need to remain incredibly ambitious - because we CAN attain them.

SAP is setting an incredibly high bar in the industry with this program, and I encourage you all to check if you are eligible to participate at least once in your career. Programs like these help us align our actions with the values we stand for and the culture we strive to uphold - where empathy and kindness can be, and are, executed in harmony with our grit and high performance.

I’ve always been proud to be an SAPer, but I’ve never been as proud as I am now!

SAP Social Sabbatical program - KenyaTaka Taka Ni MaliTaka Taka Ni Mali

entry.title: Report on SAP Signavio Process Intelligence data using the new OData API and SAP Analytics Cloud
entry.content:
Intro

With our August 2024 release, we are happy to share the general availability of our OData API to extract analytics data from SAP Signavio Process Intelligence.

SAP Signavio’s OData API is a scalable, out-of-the-box interface. It aims to make process mining data available to a broader audience and accessible in 3rd party tools other than SAP Signavio, including e.g. SAP SAP Analytics Cloud and Microsoft Excel. 

Users can integrate valuable process insights in their existing enterprise dashboards, democratizing the value of SAP Signavio Process Intelligence overall. 

Additionally, SAP’s data integration capabilities like SAP Cloud Integration within SAP Business Technology Platform can be leveraged to further ease the integration of the OData API. 

This will play a crucial part for our customers to streamline their process mining insights into central analytics and reporting tools, such as SAP Analytics or related BI solutions.

In this blog post, we would like to give you an overview of this new capability.

We will guide you through the steps on how to create the API token, then how to setup an OData view that defines which data can get extracted by the API and then ending with the actual consumption of the API in Microsoft Excel and SAP Analytics Cloud.

Create API Token

Let's start by selecting the process.

In the top right corner, select the process settings option.

When you now enter your process settings within a process of SAP Signavio Process Intelligence, you will find a new tab called "OData Views". Here you can define an API token that is required for the technical authentication against the API and also an OData view that selects the right data from your event log.

Let's start by creating a new API token. Here you can define the new name of the token and set the validity duration of the token. By default, the duration is set to 90 days.

Once you done, you can simply create your token, which then displays your token exactly once. Hence, make sure to copy it down as you will not be able to retrieve the token again once you leave the page.

Create OData View

Now we want to define the data that can be extracted with this OData API. For that we require the same SIGNAL query we use on our dashboards to query the data in our event log. In this example, I used a table widget on a dashboard and viewed it in SIGNAL mode, where I can easily copy over the SIGNAL query into my clipboard.

Now going back to my OData view tab where I created my API token beforehand, I can create an OData view.

Here I can provide a new name for the OData view, a description relating the data that can be extracted and also paste in the SIGNAL query which I copied over from the table widget in my dashboard.

Note that the column name in your SIGNAL query can't have a blank space, hence we recommend you to replace it with an underscore or letter.

Once you are done, you can click on create and you get navigated back to the OData view page, where your newly created OData view is ready to be used.

Load data into Microsoft Excel

To perform a quick test against the API before connecting it to proper enterprise applications, you can use tools such as Postman or Microsoft Excel. In this example, as it is an OData API, you can simply open a new Excel worksheet and get data via the power query option.

Here you select the option OData.

Here you need to define the URL and insert the API token as password under basic authentication. Note that the URL is built up in this format:

https://<baseUrl>/pi/signal/odata/v1/<resource>

The base URL is based on the API endpoint where your Signavio workspace is hosted. In my case the name of the OData view is "NPS_Case_Status".  The username is not important as the API token is implicitly already linked to your Signavio user. In this case, I used "unknown", but you can use any username as it will not be applied. More details on this can be found in our official documentation.

Once the API call was successful, you should see the dataset of your OData API.

Once you are happy with the data query, you can close and load the data into your excel worksheet.

Connect API to SAP Analytics Cloud

Now we want to use the API to extract data into SAP Analytics Cloud. For that we can create connection.

Here we select the OData Services option.

Here we can provide the OData Connection a name, URL and the API token, similarily to the setup in the Excel worksheet. Also here, the user name is set as mandatory, but will not be technically required for the authentication against the API. In our example, we use a Signavio workspace in the EU region, which can be seen in the respective URL, which can be found in the documentation. Again here, the user name is not required. I used "not_required", but you can use any word as it doesn't get used during authentication.

More details on this can be found in our official documentation.

Once the connection was successfully saved, we can now used it in a model.

You can use your newly created connection as a baseline for your model.

Then the system displays you all created OData Views where your user has access to. In my example, I have access to these four OData views that were able in my single process. We can select the "NPS_Case_Status" OData API and provide it a similar name.

After that we can select what columns we want to display in SAP Analytics Cloud. In our case, we would like to see all data as part of the OData API.

Once the data was successfully imported, you should be able to the newly extracted data under data foundation. Here you can use the extensive analytical capabilities in SAP Analytics Cloud to merge this model with your existing analytics artifacts.

And that is it! Let me know what you think of this new API and what you use it for. In case of questions, feel free to reach out to us.

Additional resources

SAP Help

SAP Business Accelerator Hub

August 2024 release summary

Postman Collection

Enable Now

Analytics API in SAP Signavio Process Governance

entry.title: Setup of the SAP Signavio Process Insights connector within SAP Signavio Process Intelligence
entry.content:
Great news!

With the new plug and gain connector the process of refreshing data is becoming smoother and more focused on customer needs: from now on, customers who have completed the initial set-up, up can individually take-over the “plug”-phase at any desired time, which strongly minimizes involvement of external efforts.

The SAP Signavio plug and gain is a comprehensive program designed to facilitate the rapid adoption and value realization of the SAP Signavio Process Transformation Suite for SAP ERP customers who want to move into the Cloud. The suite offers a set of tools aimed at improving business process management and optimization.

While the initial set-up of plug and gain is still delivered with support from SAP, the dependency on SAP support to actively use plug and gain on a regular basis is not required anymore. Instead, the customer has all means and abilities to conduct a data refresh whenever required, taking into account cost management with focus on available records. This drives the integration of plug and gain into our SAP Signavio suite, making it an integral part with streamlined release cycles. It is important to note that existing PnG customers cannot run the connector against their old plug and gain, it must set it up again.

Come again? What is plug and gain by SAP Signavio?

In summary, the plug and gain approach is an accelerated approach to process and ERP transformation. The plug and gain approach allows you to combine the capabilities of SAP Signavio Process Insights to measure process performance and SAP Signavio Process Intelligence to learn about actual process execution and define your to-be processes. On top of this, you are equipped with a variety of analyses to improve processes, your current ERP system and a possible target landscape.

As part of the plug and gain approach a connection between SAP ECC, SAP S/4HANA, or SAP Ariba to SAP Signavio Process Insights is established. Then, the data is loaded from SAP Signavio Process Insights to SAP Signavio Process Intelligence quickly and easily. Typical use cases for implementing plug and gain include the following:

  • To help reduce the complexity and cost of SAP S/4HANA transformation projects
  • To address challenges arising from mergers and acquisitions, resulting in distributed system landscapes and limited process harmonization
  • To handle the challenge of ERP systems that have grown historically with custom development, and obsolete configuration settings and data
  • To avoid costly process mining projects that require a lot of setup time before processes can be improved

In case you want to learn more about leveraging the plug and gain approach yet, don’t worry. You can easily catch up here. Make sure to contact your SAP counterpart to get started with plug and gain by SAP Signavio. The documentation alongside this blogpost can be found here.

How to set up the connector?

Navigate via “Process Data Management” to “Connections” and click “Create” to establish the Process Insights connection. Here, as seen in the screenshot, name the connection. Then select “SAP Signavio” as the connection type to create the connection.

Provide the requested details in the credentials inside the connection. The following hints will help you fill out the form correctly:

  • OAuth Cliend ID: the Client ID of the service key from your Process Insights API instance is required here
  • OAuth Client Secret: the Client Secret of the service key from your Process Insights API instance is required here
  • Service Root: the service link including the ending to define which service should be called
  • The service root always starts with “https://bpi-plug-and-gain.cfapps.eu11.hana.ondemand.com/api/”  and the selected service you want to connect to has to be appended to this URL.

List of available services:

  • Process Landscape Analysis: EventLogsService 
  • Invoice to Cash: Finance_I2CService
  • Invoice to Pay: Finance_I2PService
  • Acquire to Decomission: AssetManagementService
  • Source to Pay and Source to Pay incl. Ariba: SourcingAndProcurementService
  • Lead to Cash: SalesService
  • Plan to Fulfill: ManufacturingService/v1
  • Record to Report: RecordToReportService/v1

 

  • You can specify in the Service Root which service to call, so which data to be extracted from Process Insights using the connector. E.g.: URL for Source to Pay data: https://bpi-plug-and-gain.cfapps.eu11.hana.ondemand.com/api/SourcingAndProcurementService/v1/
  • OAuth Access Token URL: this URL is specific to your Process Insights Tenant URL. In general, it looks like this: https:// <YourProcessInsightsTenant>.authentication.<YourServerRegion>.hana.ondemand.com/oauth/token
  • Target Currency: Please select the main currency that you want to use in the content.

 Setting up the Process Data Pipeline:

  • You now have to access the following note and download the content that is attached. You can access the collective note over this link: https://me.sap.com/notes/3496399/E and within this note, you can select the underlying note that contains the content you want to set up
  • From here, import the Process Data Pipeline by clicking on “Import” in the “Process Data Pipelines” menu, and drag-and-drop the .etl file into the pop-up to upload the Process Data Pipeline.
  • Once the Process Data Pipeline is uploaded, the source data will be imported as well, containing all the relevant tables for the set up.

Adding the connector to the pipeline

Add a process to store your dashboards once the pipeline is set up: Click on “New Process” or reuse an existing process, provide a name, and create the process.

 

 

Preparation for the data load

  • At this point, the pipeline is all set up.
  • the source data tables required for the setup come with the import of the pipeline

Data Extraction

Before you can trigger the first data extraction over the pipeline, please make some optional adjustments to the source data tables within the source data.

By clicking on one of the tables on the arrow on the right side, you are provided with more options under “Configurations” to configure which data should be extracted

Per standard, this will be pre-configured to “1”, meaning that by default, one data collection gets loaded.

You can also specify the systems within a Process Insights tenant. This is relevant for you, if you have multiple systems connected, but you only want to extract data from one certain system. This can be done by inserting system = “Name of system” into the SQL-Filter.

Once you have made all the adjustments in the tables, you can start the initial data extraction. You can trigger this by going into the pipeline and trigger the “Run ETL” option on the top-right in the pipeline overview.

  • Once you triggered the ETL, the extraction should start.
  • Once the data has been extracted, the ETL overview should look like the below screenshot and the status should be “completed”:
  • At this point, we have extracted the data from Process Insights for the first time. Now we want to set up the content on top of the extracted data.

 Process Setup

To get started with your analysis, you now need to import the content that we provide with plug and gain. First, please import the BPMN Models and example diagrams for the Value Accelerators into your Signavio Process Manager. 

Please navigate into the Process Manager, and click “Import SAP Signavio archive (SGX)”:

Then, choose the file you want to import, in this case the BPMNs that are stored within an .sgx file. Use the options as selected in the below screenshot and import the files. These are the BPMN Models that we use in the dashboards, you will need them for the next step.

Now, you can set up the dashboards: Navigate into the pipeline again, and click on the process on the right side that you have created and attached to the pipeline:

  • Please import all the dashboards.
  • You can find the dashboards in the collective note.
  • Hint: Make sure that you are in the right tab. You want to be in the “Dashboards”-Tab within the process.

Then click import on the top-right and drag-and-drop all dashboards within this specific note.

If you now look through the imported dashboards, you might see some spots coming up where there is a process model missing or where you have to link an example diagram. As soon as you encounter this message: Please map the process model (that we have imported earlier in Step 7 into Process Manager) to the current dashboard. You can do this by clicking on the top-right three dots, into the settings. Here, you select the name of the process (e.g. Source to Pay) and get the fitting process model as a recommendation. Select the process model and it will appear in a preview as below. Then, click “Map activities”

 

Next, map the specific events from the data to the events in the process model. You can do that manually, but also automatically where matching names get assigned to each other automatically (recommended way). You can do this by selecting “Auto-map” in the top-right as seen below, and confirm the pop-up.

Now that your process is mapped to the events in the data, you can return to your dashboard and the process will be properly set up.

Another type of diagram that need to be added is example diagrams. These diagrams provide process examples for specific use cases within a dashboard. If you encounter something like this within your dashboard you have to insert this specific diagram into the dashboard. You can do that by clicking on “Create Widget” within the dashboard, and select “Give it a try” when asked if you want to switch to the new widget creation experience (only by switching, you will be able to add the example diagrams)

Then, click “Edit” by clicking on the three dots in the respective widget for the example diagram, and this side panel opens on the right. In here, type the name of the diagram (as seen in the title of the widget, here e.g.: Example: Overdelivery case charged correct), and select the diagram that is suggested. This only works after the diagram has been uploaded into the process manager via the .sgx import that was described previously.

Congratulations, you are all set!

Now you might want to take a look into access rights for the processes and the dashboards that you have created. Bear in mind that at this point, only the person that set up everything has access. Check out our documentation on to manage access rights here.

As you have extracted the data once while running the initial setup, you might want to schedule the data load automatically going forward. This way, the data in Process Insights is continuously refreshed. You can set the scheduled extraction by navigating into the source data, and click “Schedule” on the top-right. In the pop-up seen below, you can specify the schedule on which the data should be extracted, provided that the data is available in SAP Signavio Process Insights and the connection to the ERP system is still up and running.

As mentioned earlier, with the new connector the process of refreshing data is becoming smoother and more focused on user needs: From now on, plug and gain users who have completed the initial set-up, can individually take-over the “plug”-phase at any desired time. In case you are already an active plug and gain user and want to leverage the new connector, make sure to reach out to your SAP contact to get started.

If you are not an active plug and gain user yet and want to learn more about it or request a demo, you can find out more here.
